sglang.title: [PD] Vectorise group_concurrent_contiguous in NumPy
sglang.author: yuan-luo
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Improve performance of the group_concurrent_contiguous which is on the critical path of Mooncake P/D disaggregation. The following is the benchmark of the legacy and new implementation. It demonstrates a 4-5 times performance improvement.
Benchmarking with gap_prob= 0.05
N=    1000 | reps

sglang.title: Remove extra contiguous
sglang.author: ispobock
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
The contiguous will involve extra copy kernel. It's removed in #5578, but seems added back by mistake in #5383.
The input tensor does not need to be contiguous if we read the data in the kernel with the correct stride info.

vllm.title: Avoid overwriting vllm_compile_cache.py
vllm.author: youngkent
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
When vLLM is reusing a previously compile torch.compile cache files, it should not modify/overwrite it, since nothing is expected to be changed under the same hash.
After the change, verified the cache dir is no longer modified when existing cache file is found.

vllm.title: [Core] Enable IPv6 with vllm.utils.make_zmq_socket()
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This code did not previously support IPv6 addresses for a TCP
zmq socket. This fixes that, including some test cases.
I spotted this while reading #15977.
Signed-off-by: Russell Bryant rbryant@redhat.com

vllm.title: [Frontend] Show progress bar for adding requests
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
When performing offline batched inference over a large number of images, vLLM appears to hang because it takes a long time to preprocess all of the images before the model is run. This PR adds a progress bar to eliminate this confusion.
Example with long preprocessing time
import numpy as np
from PI

VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER=0 VLLM_USE_TRITON_FLASH_ATTN=0 python3 benchmarks/benchmark_throughput.py --input-len $_in_len --output-len $_out_len --trust-remote-code --num-prompts 200 --model Qwen/Qwen3-30B-A3B-FP8 --max-

vllm.title: Fix more broken speculative decode tests

vllm.bodyExcerpt:
A follow-up PR to fix some more speculative decode tests from #17084.  There are 2 fixes:

After #17084, there are now two sampler objects:

https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/blob/main/vllm/spec_decode/multi_step_worker.py#L53
and https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/blob/main/vllm/model_executor

vllm.title: [doc] add streamlit integration
vllm.author: reidliu41
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Streamlit lets you transform Python scripts into interactive web apps in minutes, instead of weeks.
So it is very easy to create chat app, and make vllm as backend server to test, or make a demo.
It would be helpful to add an example of this integration to the documentation.

vllm.title: [FEAT] [ROCm]: Add Qwen/Qwen3-235B-A22B-FP8 TP4 triton fused moe config

VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER=0 VLLM_USE_TRITON_FLASH_ATTN=0 python3 benchmarks/benchmark_throughput.py --input-len $_in_len --output-len $_out_len --trust-remote-code --num-prompts 200 --model Qwen/Qwen3-235B-A22B-FP8 --max-model-len 32768 --gpu_memory

vllm.title: [ROCm] remove unsupported archs from rocm triton flash-attention supported list
vllm.author: hongxiayang
vllm.bodyExcerpt: gfx940 and gfx941 are no longer supported since ROCm 6.3, and also they are not in the PyTorch arch list (env PYTORCH_ROCM_ARCH) . This simple clean up PR is to remove them from the code (here the triton flash-attention supported list).

vllm.author: SageMoore
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Credit to @LucasWilkinson for the kernel.
This pass currently only supports static per-tensor quantization. Other quantization schemes will be included in a subsequent PRs.

vllm.title: [Misc]add configurable cuda graph size
vllm.author: CXIAAAAA
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
add configurable cuda graph size, default to 512
could save 1-2g in some extreme mem saving case, e.g. 128 or smaller
usage:

--cuda-graph-sizes 1 128 8, it will be sorted first and just capture, 1/8/128
--cuda-graph-sizes 512, it will capture based on the logic: [1, 2, 4] + [i for i in range(8, cud

vllm.title: [Misc][Tools][Benchmark] Publish script to auto tune server parameters
vllm.author: Chenyaaang
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Create a script to auto tune server parameter.
This script aims to tune the best server parameter combinations to maximize throughput for given requirement. The current server parameter combination is  max_num_seqs and max_num_batched_tokens. It also supports additional requirement: e2e latency and

Test plan:

offline:


VLLM_TORCH_PROFILER_DIR=/data/users/qizixi/gitrepos/vllm/profiles VLLM_USE_V1=1 python examples/offline_inference/eagle.py  --num_spec_tokens 3 --ma

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Disable gptq_bitblas for <SM80 to fix GPTQ on V100/T4
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
FIX #17539
FIX #17367
As the attached issue found, gptq_bitblas doesn't seem to work at the moment on NVIDIA T4. This causes GPTQ models to fail to run as the override gets triggered without this change. Let's disable this for now
cc @LeiWang1999

vllm.title: Add `pt_load_map_location` to allow loading to cuda
vllm.author: jerryzh168
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary:
Right now pytorch checkpoints are always loaded to cpu device, but some checkpoints can only be loaded in cuda or other devices, this PR adds a pt_load_map_location flag (defaults to cpu) to LoadConfig
Test Plan:
python tests/test_config.py -k test_load_config_pt_load_map_location

python b
